channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Foo Fighters Share Humorous Stories and Embarrassing Lyrics on Hot Ones Versus
item.description: Foo Fighters recently appeared on the spin-off interview series Hot Ones Versus, where the band members faced both spicy wings and challenging questions. The group discussed a variety of personal anec...

item.title: Daniel Dae Kim Finds 'Lost' Series Finale Satisfying Due to Character Closure
item.description: Actor Daniel Dae Kim, best known for his role as Jin-Soo Kwon in the acclaimed ABC series 'Lost,' recently shared his positive perspective on the show's highly debated 2010 series finale. In an interv...

item.title: Understanding U.S. Support for Israel: Faith, Politics and Strategy
item.description: The article discusses how American support for Israel is influenced not only by geopolitical strategy but also by religious and political beliefs, particularly among Evangelical Christians. It highlig...

item.title: Shark Activity Increases Near Sydney Beaches, Swimmers Advised to Take Caution
item.description: Authorities in Sydney have issued warnings for beachgoers as recent conditions in local waters are attracting both bull sharks and great white sharks closer to shore. Experts advise that warmer waters...

item.title: Wes Streeting Resigns, Urges Labour to Tackle Nationalism and Youth Challenges
item.description: Wes Streeting, former Health Secretary and Labour MP for Ilford North, has resigned from the cabinet, citing a loss of confidence in Sir Keir Starmer's leadership. In a Commons speech, Streeting warne...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch criticised for errors in PMQs over fuel duty and sanctions
item.description: During Prime Minister's Questions, Kemi Badenoch, leader of the opposition, faced criticism for demonstrating a lack of understanding on key political and international issues. She inaccurately claime...

item.title: SpaceX Files for IPO, Potentially Becoming World's First Trillion-Dollar Public Company
item.description: SpaceX, the aerospace company founded by Elon Musk in 2002, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO), potentially marking the first U.S. market debut valued at over US$1 trillion. Known for its ...

item.title: Celebrating Irene Vanbrugh: Icon of British Theatre
item.description: Dame Irene Vanbrugh (1872–1949) was a legendary figure in British theatre, admired for her extraordinary talent and commanding presence on stage. Over a career lasting more than fifty years, she becam...

item.title: Nicolas Cage’s Costume Tests for Tim Burton’s Cancelled Superman Film
item.description: In the mid-1990s, Warner Bros. planned a bold reboot of the Superman franchise called 'Superman Lives,' directed by Tim Burton and starring Nicolas Cage as Clark Kent. The film, inspired by the 'Death...

item.title: eThekwini Municipality reviews R6.4bn irregular expenditure, pledges improved governance
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality is facing intense scrutiny after over R6.4 billion was recorded as irregular expenditure across the 2023/24 and 2024/25 financial years. The revelations came during the muni...

item.title: Tanzania Warns Middle East Tensions Could Disrupt Fuel, Food, and Tourism
item.description: Tanzania has raised alarm about the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, warning that it could seriously affect African economies despite the distance. Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba highlighted that ...

item.title: Adan Mohamed Takes Office as KRA Commissioner General Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: Nairobi – Adan Mohamed has officially been sworn in as the new Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) commissioner-general, despite ongoing court cases seeking to halt his appointment. The ceremony took place ...

item.title: Ontario resident under medical evaluation for Ebola following travel to East Africa
item.description: An Ontario resident who recently returned from East Africa is currently undergoing testing for Ebola, according to the province's Ministry of Health. The patient is being assessed in hospital, and hea...

item.title: Supreme Court clarifies bail principles under UAPA, emphasising constitutional liberty
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has reignited a crucial debate on personal liberty under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA). In a May 18 judgment, a bench of Justices BV Nagarathna and Ujjal B...

item.title: Mbalula appoints 39-member task team to manage Eastern Cape ANC amid court challenges
item.description: ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula has appointed a 39-member provincial task team to oversee the Eastern Cape ANC until its next elective conference. This move follows the dissolution of the provinc...

item.title: WA experiences first diphtheria outbreak in 50 years, authorities urge vaccinations
item.description: Western Australia is facing its first outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in about 50 years, with hundreds of cases reported across WA, Northern Territory, Queensland, and South Australia. The outbreak...

item.title: Philippine Air Force trainer plane crashes in Benguet, 2 pilots confirmed dead
item.description: Two Philippine Air Force (PAF) pilots lost their lives after an SF-260 trainer aircraft crashed in the forested area of Barangay Cabuyao, Tuba, Benguet on the morning of May 20, 2026. The plane, part ...

item.title: APC Concludes Primaries in Delta State, Produces Candidates for All 29 Assembly Seats
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don wrap up their primary elections for Delta State House of Assembly, and dem don produce candidates for all 29 constituencies ahead of the 2027 general elections....

item.title: RAF spy plane experiences multiple risky interceptions by Russian jets over Black Sea
item.description: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) has reported that two Russian warplanes engaged in multiple dangerous interceptions of an unarmed Royal Air Force (RAF) Rivet Joint surveillance aircraft over the Black S...

item.title: Amazon Ends Support for Older Kindle Devices: Options for Users
item.description: Amazon has announced that, as of May 20, 2026, many older Kindle devices will no longer be able to purchase, borrow, or download new content from the Kindle Store. This includes almost all Kindle mode...
